# Feature: History Real-Time Refresh ‚Äî Auto-Reload After Persistence

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-007
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

The History pane shows stale data: sending a request persists it to history (`storage.saveRequestToHistory` in `handleSendRequest`, e.g., [vscode App.tsx:354](../../packages/vscode/src/webview/App.tsx)), but `setHistory` is only called at initial load and on manual retry ([App.tsx:150-160, 236-245](../../packages/vscode/src/webview/App.tsx)) ‚Äî new entries appear only after a full reload. Fix: after each successful history persistence, reload history through the adapter and update the slice, following the app's established pull-based refresh pattern (mutate via adapter ‚Üí reload via adapter ‚Üí `set*` slice; collections and cookies already do this).

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No data-model changes. Behavioral contract:

### Entities & Attributes

- **`refreshHistory()` helper** (one per `App.tsx`, same shape as `handleRetryHistory` minus the loading-state toggles): `storage.loadHistory()` ‚Üí on `ok` `setHistory(value)`, on `err` log + `setHistoryLoadError` is **not** set (a background refresh failure must not blank a working pane ‚Äî show nothing or a console warning; the pane keeps its last data).
- Call sites: after a successful `saveRequestToHistory` in `handleSendRequest` (both platforms), and after any other flow found mutating history persistence without updating the slice (TASK-003 audit).

### Relationships

- Pull-based refresh only ‚Äî no event bus, no subscription (plan architecture rule).

### Indexes & Constraints

- The refresh is silent: no `setIsHistoryLoading(true)` flash during background refresh (loading states remain for initial load and manual retry only).

## 3. Files

- `packages/vscode/src/webview/App.tsx` ‚Äî `refreshHistory` helper + call after save
- `packages/web/src/App.tsx` ‚Äî same
- `packages/core/src/components/common/HistoryPane.tsx` (audit; touch only if a stale flow is found)
- Existing App-level / HistoryPane tests ‚Äî extend where present
- `docs/features/requests.md` or `docs/features/settings.md` ‚Äî N/A (behavioral fix; no doc change beyond release notes)

## 4. Dependencies

- None.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: vscode ‚Äî refresh history after send

- [x] **Implementation**: In `packages/vscode/src/webview/App.tsx`, extract `refreshHistory()` (load ‚Üí `setHistory` on ok; on error, log with context and keep current pane state ‚Äî do not set `historyLoadError`). In `handleSendRequest`, after `await storage.saveRequestToHistory(tabRequest)` succeeds (`result.isOk` ‚Äî check the Result; currently the return value is ignored), call `await refreshHistory()`. Keep `handleRetryHistory` as-is (it owns the loading/error states) or reimplement it as loading-wrapped `refreshHistory` to avoid duplication.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Test (mock adapter via `createMockAdapter`) ‚Äî sending a request triggers `loadHistory` after `saveRequestToHistory` and the store's history contains the new entry (**regression-first**: this test fails on current code); `loadHistory` failure after save leaves previous history intact and does not render the pane's error state.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: Inline comment on `refreshHistory` referencing the pull-based refresh pattern.
- **Verify**: Run the extension, send a request with the History pane open ‚Äî the entry appears immediately, no reload.

### TASK-002: web ‚Äî refresh history after send

- [x] **Implementation**: Mirror TASK-001 in `packages/web/src/App.tsx` (same helper, same call site in its `handleSendRequest`).
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Same assertions against the web `App` (or its handler hook) with the mock adapter.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None (same comment pattern).
- **Verify**: Run the web app, send a request ‚Äî History pane updates immediately.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001 (reuse the identical helper shape)

### TASK-003: Audit clear/delete history flows for slice staleness

- [x] **Implementation**: Audit `HistoryPane` callers and both `App.tsx` files for any flow that mutates history persistence without updating the slice: single delete (plan notes `removeHistoryItem` already covers it ‚Äî confirm), clear-all (if a `clearHistory` flow exists), and the post-response cookie-style flows. Wire any stale flow to `refreshHistory()` (or the appropriate slice action). If nothing is stale, record that in the implementation notes ‚Äî this task may legitimately be a no-op beyond tests.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: delete-item flow leaves store consistent with persistence (existing behavior pinned by a test if none exists).
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None.
- **Verify**: Delete a history entry, send a new request, reload the app ‚Äî pane contents identical before/after reload (no divergence between store and disk).
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: `saveRequestToHistory` and `loadHistory` return `Result`; check both. Background-refresh failure is logged, never thrown, and never clobbers the visible pane with an error state (manual retry remains the recovery path).
- **Logging**: Log background refresh failures with operation context (`refreshHistory after send`). Never log request contents (may contain secrets in headers/body).
